text: In this biennial review of atomic-weight determinations and related isotopic data, we present revised standard atomic weights for five chemical elements. Significant updates include the reclassification of bromine from 79.904(1) to the interval [79.901, 79.907] and magnesium from 24.3050(6) to [24.304, 24.307]. These shifts to interval notation reflect the natural variations in isotopic abundance found across common terrestrial materials. Furthermore, the standard atomic weights for germanium, indium, and mercury have been refined to 72.630(8), 114.818(1), and 200.592(3), respectively, providing greater precision for the scientific community.
